title: Pre-monsoon showers, thunderstorms forecast across Maharashtra; IMD issues alerts - Moneycontrol.com
full_text:
After days of scorching temperatures, parts of Maharashtra are likely to get some relief as pre-monsoon activity intensifies across the state. However, heatwave conditions are expected to persist in several districts even as rainfall and thunderstorms become more frequent.
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a yellow rain alert for all districts in the Konkan region, while yellow heat alerts remain in force for some parts of the state. The changing weather pattern is expected to bring a mix of rainfall, thunderstorms, lightning, and hot, humid conditions across different regions, News18 reported.
According to the forecast, districts in the Konkan belt, including Palghar, Thane, Mumbai, Raigad, and Ratnagiri, are likely to experience hot weather during the day followed by light rainfall later in the afternoon. Sindhudurg may also receive light showers as the region remains under a yellow rain alert.
In North Maharashtra, weather conditions are expected to vary. While Dhule is likely to remain dry, Nandurbar and Jalgaon may witness hot and humid conditions. Light rainfall has been forecast for Nashik and Ahilyanagar, offering some respite from the prevailing heat.
Western Maharashtra is expected to see increased rainfall activity. Pune may receive light to moderate rain, while Kolhapur, Satara, Sangli, and Solapur are likely to witness thunderstorms accompanied by lightning. The IMD has issued a yellow alert for these districts due to unstable weather conditions, according to News18 report.
In the Marathwada region, Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar and Jalna remain under a yellow heat alert as temperatures are expected to stay high. Light rain is likely in Parbhani and Hingoli, while Beed, Nanded, Latur and Dharashiv could receive light to moderate showers.
Vidarbha is expected to witness contrasting weather conditions, with heatwave conditions likely in Amravati and thunderstorms accompanied by gusty winds forecast for Bhandara, Chandrapur, Gadchiroli, Gondia, Nagpur, Wardha and Yavatmal. No alerts have been issued for Washim and Buldhana districts.
Meanwhile, neighbouring Goa has issued a monsoon safety advisory as the southwest monsoon advances. Authorities have urged residents and tourists to avoid entering the sea and follow safety guidelines due to rough weather conditions and dangerous currents.
With rainfall, thunderstorms, and heatwave conditions expected simultaneously across different parts of Maharashtra, residents have been advised to stay updated with the latest weather bulletins and follow local advisories, as per News18.
